ideas.concentration: Intuition behind why gradient descent moves in the direction of the negative gradient
ideas.rationale: Helps learners connect the mathematical definition of gradient to its practical role in minimizing loss—critical for building intuition before diving into algorithms.

ideas.concentration: Practical effect of learning rate too high vs too low in real-world training
ideas.rationale: Reinforces a common real-world pitfall—students often struggle with hyperparameter tuning; this question grounds theory in observable training behavior.

ideas.concentration: Why gradient descent is preferred over brute-force search in neural networks
ideas.rationale: Highlights scalability and efficiency—key reasons gradient descent is used in practice, contrasting theoretical vs practical optimization approaches.

ideas.concentration: Visualizing gradient descent on a simple 2D loss surface (e.g., parabola) with different starting points
ideas.rationale: Builds spatial intuition for convergence and local minima—helps learners anticipate behavior without needing to code or compute derivatives.

ideas.concentration: How stochastic gradient descent (SGD) improves training speed in large datasets compared to batch GD
ideas.rationale: Connects theory to modern ML practice—SGD is the default in most frameworks; this question clarifies why it's not just a variant but a necessity for scalability.

selected_ideas.concentration: Intuition behind why gradient descent moves in the direction of the negative gradient
selected_ideas.rationale: Foundational for building intuition—learners must understand why the negative gradient points toward minimization before applying it practically.

selected_ideas.concentration: Practical effect of learning rate too high vs too low in real-world training
selected_ideas.rationale: Directly addresses a ubiquitous real-world challenge in optimization, grounding theory in observable training behavior learners encounter daily.

feedback: All ideas have valid question_type 'choice'. Top two ideas are selected based on strong alignment with user preference for intuition and practical applications, and good diversity: idea_1 builds foundational intuition about the negative gradient, while idea_2 addresses a critical real-world tuning issue. Idea_5 is a strong contender but overlaps slightly with idea_2 in practical focus; idea_4 is visually intuitive but choice format may limit depth. Selected pair balances theory-to-practice transition and common pitfalls.
